title: Project Description
description: Input specification for the Agent Teams build_team pipeline. Describes the project, its deliverables, tools, and components to generate a complete agent team.

project_name.description: Short, slug-safe project name (e.g., 'MyResearchProject', 'DataPipelineV2'). If absent, inferred from the project directory name.

project_goal.description: 1–3 sentence description of what the project produces and why. Used as {PROJECT_GOAL} throughout all agent files.

deliverables.description: List of primary deliverable types this project produces.

output_format.description: The final output format for compiled deliverables (e.g., 'HTML5', 'PDF', 'Python 3.11 modules', 'LaTeX'). Used as {OUTPUT_FORMAT}.

primary_output_dir.description: Directory where primary authored deliverables are written, relative to project root (e.g., 'src/', 'html/chapters/', 'docs/'). Used as {PRIMARY_OUTPUT_DIR}.

build_output_dir.description: Directory where compiled/converted output artifacts go (e.g., 'build/', 'dist/', 'manuscript/'). Used as {BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}.

figures_dir.description: Directory for diagrams and figures. Used as {FIGURES_DIR}.

existing_project_path.description: Absolute path to an existing project directory on disk. If provided, the analyzer scans it for structure, tools, and potential authority sources.

agents_output_dir.description: Directory where generated agent files will be written. Defaults to '.github/agents/' within the project path.

authority_sources.description: Files or directories that agents must treat as ground truth. Used to build {AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY} and {AUTHORITY_SOURCES_LIST}.

name.description: Human-readable name of this authority source.

path.description: File or directory path, relative to project root or absolute.

scope.description: What types of claims this source is authoritative for.

rank.description: Priority rank in the authority hierarchy (1 = highest).

style_reference.description: Path to the style guide, voice samples, or editorial conventions file. Used as {STYLE_REFERENCE_PATH}.

reference_db_path.description: Path to the bibliography or reference database (BibTeX, CSV, JSON, Markdown). Used as {REFERENCE_DB_PATH}.

reference_key_convention.description: Citation key format convention. Used as {REFERENCE_KEY_CONVENTION}.

tools.description: Languages, frameworks, and tools the project uses.

config_files.description: Config files associated with this tool.

needs_specialist_agent.description: If true, generate a dedicated tool-specific agent for this tool.

style_rules.description: Project-specific style rules in plain English. Used in copilot-instructions.md.

selected_archetypes.description: Override the auto-detected archetype selection. If absent, the analyzer selects archetypes from the project description.

components.description: Workstream components. One workstream expert agent is generated per component.

name.description: Human-readable name of this component (e.g., 'Chapter 1: Introduction').

output_file.description: Path to this component's primary output file, relative to primary_output_dir.

description.description: 1–3 sentences describing what this component produces.

sections.description: List of sections, functions, or sub-units in this component.

sources.description: Known source references (citation keys or file paths) relevant to this component.

cross_refs.description: Other component slugs this component references.

quality_criteria.description: Pass/fail quality criteria the workstream expert will check against in reviews.

conversion_pipeline.description: Command or tool used to convert primary deliverables to output format. Used as {CONVERSION_PIPELINE} in format-converter agent.
